Cyberlink Director Suite 6 offers an easy to use product that is perfect for entry level and casual video editors, while offering some robust features for even the most seasoned professional. Even my 10 year old child can't just jump right in and get working. It is that wasy.

In the past, I've always found Cyberlink software kind of cheap and fly by night. That is no longer the case. While not as polished as some other editors I've used, the interface is now very stream lined and much simpler to use. As mentioned, my daughter was able to jump right in and get working. I started her out with the old but tried and true Windows Movie Maker but then had her try this and she was up, running and making video clips in no time.

Video editing was a joy so much so that I may use this full time. Importing video from video files was very simple. For legacy (8MM or VHS-C) I wasn't able to import via a firewire video camera. It just wouldn't detect the camera. Once the video was imported, all of the tools needed to cut, edit and add transitions were easy to find and implement. Sometimes I'd have to go back and fill gaps when the auto gap fill wasn't working but it wasn't a biggie.

Having both of these bundling is a fantastic value and I would choose this over competing brands. This is an easy to use and powerful program and I can easily recommend it over competing brands.

I want to jump right in and say that I love this mouse. It is comfortable, responsive and has really goes well with my Alienware Mechanical Gaming Keyboard. Beyond a few issues, this is my new daily mouse on my main PC.

Setup is pretty much plug and play. You pop the wireless receiver into an available USB port and it installs the needed drivers. On my Dell PC, is quietly installed or activated the Alienware Software that will let you fine tune your mouse and color scheme.

The mouse is a joy to use. It is comfortable and responsive. For day to day use, it is great but it does excel in gaming with it's programmable buttons and great location of the extra buttons. I'm a huge fan of the much maligned Fallout 76 and I love being able to quickly get to my most used options in this game. Mouse precision is also quite good and it is starting to convert me from a melee player to using more guns.

I have a few cons. The biggest for me is the scrolling of the mouse wheel. While it works good in games, it isn't "fine" enough for web browsing or Microsoft Office, even with the settings adjustments. I often have issues with the wireless transmitter where the mouse occasionally gets jumpy or more often, it will say the battery is critically low when it isn't. But none of these are show stoppers and these are things that could be addressed in future firmware updates.

This is a great mouse and if you use and trust Alienware products, you NEED this mouse.

I've been a wireless keyboard mouse user for a few years now. I've had moments of frustration with lost connections with wireless dongles or Bluetooth just seizing up. It is a shame because not only was it nice to have no wires, they keyboards I used were pretty comfortable. So, taking a little step back and trying a wired keyboard for both work and gaming. After using the Alienware AW510K, I don't think I'll go wireless again.

Setting up the Alienware AW510K is super easy. It will require two USB ports, one for powering the lights and the other for connectivity. I connected it to two different PC's and on the second one, it automatically downloaded the Alienware Command Center. Not sure why the first one didn't but it is available in the Microsoft Store on your Windows 10 PC. Once in the software, it is a little difficult at first to determine where and how to change the lighting but once set, it is easy to find again.

The Alienware AW510K is a super nice looking keyboard and feels amazing. It has some weight to it but it feels solid. I love the volume rocker and I forgotten how good a mechanical keyboard feels. The keys are easy to press and has that satisfying click. The keyboard is just as fun to use for work as it is gaming.

I just come out and say it...I love this keyboard. It is durable, comfortable and a joy to use.

user_history_reviews.review_text: I've owned several incarnations of Rosetta Stone in the past and found it a favorable experience. The frustration was the format, being on CDs made portability and utility a challenge. Now more than ever, since I switched to MacBook Pro laptops long ago that lack a built in CD player and so require lugging around one more additional component and setup. Rosetta Stone also has timed subscriptions for a given time, say one or two years. That should be sufficient as long as you put your most to the grindstone and also don't need to revisit the lessons in the future for review. A life long streaming program like this is really the way to go for convenience and accessibility. Access your lessons on a computer, tablet or your phone from anywhere, or download a lesson for use where wifi is not available. Some complained about speech recognition accuracy. Personally, I haven't had any problems, at least so far. At the time of this review, the package was lowered to under $100. At that price, especially with the inclusion of a massive dictionary and grammar guide, I think it offers an outstanding value. There is no magic wand to learning a language. It will always require perseverance and repetition over a period of time. I personally have a harder time than others who seem to have brains that are more retentive to language. But I like the Rosetta Stone premise. Its not easy and can be frustrating at times. But I believe it is the best resource available short of traveling to and living in the country the language is spoken or having a very expensive private tutor.

Packed with depth rarely, if ever seen in a simulation game developed for a handheld, and utilizing the functionality of the DS' stylus and touchscreen, The Sims 3 allows players to actually craft the lives of their Sims, rather than simply follow predetermined paths. AFKPKKYN6747GTCEC62R5FNJDZDA.product_description: The Turtle Beach Stealth 600 is the latest wireless surround sound gaming headset for PS5 & PS4. Debuting an all-new modern style, the Stealth 600 delivers virtual surround sound plus chat audio through powerful 50mm over-ear speakers, and features Turtle Beach’s renowned high-sensitivity mic that can be flipped-up to mute. For comfort, the Stealth 600 dawns the latest iteration of Turtle Beach’s unique ProSpecs glasses friendly design and has breathable mesh fabric-wrapped headband and ear-cushions. The Stealth 600 also includes Turtle Beach’s exclusive Superhuman Hearing sound setting, Variable Mic Monitoring, audio presets including Bass Boost, and a 15-hour rechargeable battery…all for an MSRP of $99.95 Sony has indicated that PS4 headsets will work on PS5. Turtle Beach anticipates the Stealth 600 will be compatible with PS5 and will confirm once testing is complete.
AFKPKKYN6747GTCEC62R5FNJDZDA.review_text:
Playing games on your large TV provides a great visual experience but sound has always been a mixed bag and the provided headsets that some game system provided just didn't work well. The Turtle Beach Stealth 600 provides the missing elements to take your game-play to the next level.

The Turtle Beach Stealth 600 headset is very easy to set up. Just pair from the system menus of the XBOX and you are ready. They also reconnect automatically each time your power them on.

The sound quality is quite good. I am an avid Fallout 76 player and I was surprised to hear that the Scorched enemies are actually trying to talk and you can hear mumbled words. In the game ALIEN: Isolation, you hear a lot more of the alien crawling closer to you and that helps with your strategy. Microphone also picks up well, even if the stem is short.

The headsets aren't altogether comfortable. While it is easy to adjust to get a good fit, they just don't feel as tight over the ears as they can be. I often find myself having to readjust to get that "good fit" back again.

I do recommend the Turtle Beach Stealth 600. It takes your gaming to a whole new level.

AGBDHFQ75RBPKRGOB7KJT2EPD7WA.review_text:
[[VIDEOID:33caa6e2e928a4d7d75315c7dc34bc6]] Bought in April 2021 and reviewing in August 2021.

I tried for so long to get one of these. I finally secured one in April of 2021 at 3am. So for buyers experience I would give this a 1 star. But for the system itself I give a 5.

For games at the time of writing this there is nothing good right now besides Astros Playroom which takes full advantage of the controller which is pretty cool and I just can't describe in words. You have to have hands on experience to understand but it's definitely worth it.

If you own a ps4 and had bought disks previously than I suggest getting this disk version. Otherwise there's no point. Because even if you buy a game on disk you still have to install the whole game on the hard drive but then also need the disk to play which defeats the purpose of buying a disk to save space. The only other reason to want a disk version is if you don't want have updates in a game. The only way to go back to 1.0 for example is to uninstall then reinstall with a disk in offline mode and not update.

As far as space this actually sucks. I had an external drive for my ps4 already so I was able to keep my ps4 games on that and easily just plug into the ps5 and launch from there. But you can't do that with the ps5 yet. And to get any decent internal space on the ps5 is going to cost way more than the ps5 itself so that's a problem.

Overall I still love the ps5. And the controller is awesome with games it's compatible with. Can't wait for more games to come out in the future. Also the controller I feel like is the best one yet. I even bought an extra to use with my pc for gaming.

AGEQI5OZD7JWXVRRGXBO5ZRN6IUQ.product_description: Product Description Amazon Basics Controller Charging Station for PlayStation 4 DualShock 4 Controllers - 4 Foot Cable, Black From the Manufacturer Amazon Basics
AGEQI5OZD7JWXVRRGXBO5ZRN6IUQ.review_text: If you have a PS4 it's almost a no-brainer to get a charging station. The controllers still use micro-USB cables while most newer devices and phones use the USB-C cables for charging and I have both types of cables around. When I wanted to charge my controller the first annoying hurdle was finding the right cable, examining the end and then plugging it in. Having a dedicated station with a small footprint that sits next to the PS4 really makes things fast and simple. The controller just clicks into place and starts charging. When it's full, the circuitry prevents it from overcharging. I have a charging station for my PS3 as well which worked great but required a small adaptor be inserted into the controller charge port so it made proper contact with the charging base. AGEQI5OZD7JWXVRRGXBO5ZRN6IUQ.product_description: Product Description Amazon Basics PlayStation 4 Controller Charging Cable - Pack of 2, 6 Foot, Blue From the Manufacturer Amazon Basics
AGEQI5OZD7JWXVRRGXBO5ZRN6IUQ.review_text: Amazon basics, for anyone unaware, are mainstream products like cables and cases that Amazon produces under it's own brand incorporating an attractive price point with quality construction. When I saw they were offering what they call a PS4 controller charging cable, it was slightly bewildering, since any micro USB to USB cable will accomplish this task without any special modifications or bells and whistles. So why would you need to purchase this? In a word, or actually 2 words, you don't. That being said, this does have merit and logic as a discreet product. Personally, I have some phones and tablets that now use the micro USB-c type plug with its more logical uniform shape plug eliminating the need to poke and then reverse the position of the plug in your effort to insert it and make a connection, as well all devices with the older micro-USB plug that the PS4 controller has. It's an easy process but none the less a small pain to need to grab a cable and then determine if its the old or the newer type USB. Having a designated cable just for the PS4, perhaps stored right next to your machine, with its unique shade of blue color, insures you don't need to fumble or go on a cable hunt each time you need to charge your controller. The cable itself is very well made with black colored, non slip moulded ends that are beefy and easy to hold as well as sturdy. There's also a slight bevel in the sides of the ends that allow your fingers to rest inside the slight convex, to give you leverage when inserting or removing them. The cable is a a functional, though not overly generous 6', which allows you to continue playing your game even after the low power warning sneaks up on you, while not being so long that when you don't need that length to keep playing, the cord doesn't seem ridiculously overkill and in your way if just charging quietly on its own. The diameter of the insulation is sufficient to help keep it rigid and not adopt weird tangles. A small thing to make your life a tiny bid less complicated and always at the ready to charge your controller or allow you to keep the game play going.

The grips sit very high, that would have taken some getting used to anyway. But sadly, these grips are a complete bust. AG6Q4IOBTHWH5J74PHNAPYKILECA.product_description: 1 x Nintendo 3DS XL Circle Pad Pro Controller (Japanese Version). AG6Q4IOBTHWH5J74PHNAPYKILECA.review_text:
I purchased the Japanese import of the circle pad pro XL because I wanted to get the most out of all of my [[ASIN:B008GEH9HO Nintendo 3DS]] games (and didn't feel like waiting for US release). Note that the circle pad pro LL/XL fits the American XL DS. If you don't own an XL you will need the [[ASIN:B006J66CPG this one]].

It is pretty easy to use. It takes a single AAA battery (which it comes with), and the battery seems to last a long time. You can unscrew the battery compartment with a key or a coin if it needs replacing. The 3DS XL snaps into place easily. The second circle pad is as smooth and comfortable as the first. Most of the games that support it seem to come with calibration routines in case it doesn't seem to be working correctly (I haven't had a problem yet). The right side includes additional right trigger buttons since you can no longer reach the right buttons, and adds a second set of triggers to both sides.

I find the ergonomics of the device to be quite good. It fits very comfortable in my hands, and I'll sometimes leave it on when I'm not playing compatible games.

There are a few things that are worth noting before you purchase:
1. Do your research. This only works for a few games, and does different things depending on the game. For instance, with [[ASIN:B002I0EMBI Kid Icarus: Uprising]] it _only_ makes the game more accessible for lefties. (I hoped that it would eliminate the need for the stylus, which it does not do). However for [[ASIN:B009EWHKAI Monster Hunter 3 Ultimate]] (the 3DS version) it makes gameplay a lot better by eliminating the need for a virtual D-pad. It also makes underwater fighting a lot easier.

2. The CPP doesn't seem to be hot-swappable. As far as I've been able to tell you have to start up the game with the CPP. If you close your DS to put it to sleep it will sometimes complain about losing the connection (it connects with the IR port, not a hard connection). I've found if you use the CPP buttons as soon as you wake the device it will usually find it. This means that you may have to restart your game if your DS goes to sleep and doesn't recognize the CPP or if you need to take it off and put it back on (see #3).

3. I find it quite surprising that the unit completely blocks the stylus and doesn't provide an alternate stylus. This seems like a poor design choice. The CPP has enough girth that I suspect there is room for another stylus port in the device, but it doesn't provide one. If Nintendo happens to be listening this would be clear way to improve the device.

If it included a second stylus port I would give at least four stars, but this omission seriously detracts from the design/playability.

Related to point 1 I also wish that more game designers (Nintendo included) would have focused on making the games enhanced or at least compatible with the CPP.

Once I got past the mandatory Ubisoft account garbage I had quite a bit of fun for a number of hours fun. A lot of fun exploration to be had despite a relatively empty and dead world.

I just cannot get over the vaults/mini dungeons. Somehow in copying the mechanic from BOTW they forgot to make them fun. They are boring, tedious, and overly time-consuming. Some are so notoriously badly written that entire forum discussions are devoted to them.

Turns out I started avoiding them altogether and when I finally started entering them again to unlock further gods I just lost so much energy that I had to retire the game completely.

It is a shame because the idea of completing the game was initially very appealing.
